title: ARCH_INJECTION_SPEC
description: Architectural modification spec for weight_transfer.py. Pointed to by ARCH_INJECTION_SPEC_FILE in train.py Section ②. Consumed by build_custom_model_with_injection() at runtime.

mode.description: Strategy selector. 'insertions' is the v1.7 supported mode (weight_transfer programmatically generates a new YAML from a list of insertions). 'full_yaml' is reserved for v1.8+ (agent writes a complete YAML; helper only computes layer_map and transfers). Using 'full_yaml' in v1.7 raises NotImplementedError.

insertions.description: List of module insertions to apply to the base model's YAML. Multiple insertions at different positions are allowed. Helper processes them in descending-index order to keep base-YAML indices stable.

strict.description: Per-entry strict mode. When true, every entry in the computed layer_map must transfer at least one tensor, otherwise weight_transfer raises. Disable only if you know some custom layers genuinely have no counterpart in the pretrained model.

layer_map_override.description: Reserved for v1.8. When null, helper auto-computes via class-name pairing. When dict, keys/values are stringified integers.

module_class.description: Class name as it will appear in the generated YAML and in custom_modules.py's register_custom_modules() registry. Must be a Lazy* wrapper (see train-script-spec.md § Lazy-wrapper contract).

oneOf.description: Insert after every occurrence of a layer of class `class_name` within the given `scope`. e.g. after_class='Conv' + scope='backbone' inserts after every Conv in the backbone.

index.description: Index in the BASE YAML, not the generated custom YAML. Helper computes offsets internally.

oneOf.description: Insert after the layer at the given index in the base YAML. Helper validates that this index falls within `scope`; raises if out of scope.

scope.description: Which part of the YAML to modify. 'neck' and 'head' are split by position relative to the first Detect layer (head = Detect + anything feeding directly into it; neck = everything between backbone end and head). When position.kind='at_index', scope is enforced as an assertion: the index must fall within scope.

yaml_args.description: Positional args for the YAML line, e.g. [64] for [-1, 1, LazyCBAM, [64]]. The first element is conventionally the channel count; Lazy* wrappers ignore it and read x.shape[1] at forward time (see train-script-spec.md § Lazy-wrapper contract). Still required for parse_model's c2 computation.

module_kwargs.description: Keyword args to pass to the Lazy* wrapper's __init__ via the YAML line. Not commonly used — most hyperparameters go in yaml_args. Reserved for wrappers that explicitly accept **kwargs.
